methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $95M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Causaly is a Series B–stage biotech and health company that has raised $95M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 5 private peers in the same sector and stage (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent), alongside 3 public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) for directional context.

Two quantitative methods were triangulated. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $95M total raised, implying a $180M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 5 same-sector, same-stage companies yields a median valuation of $810M, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 7.8x across the three public peers) is not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

Synthesizing the two applicable methods produces a triangulated range of $153M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $495M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables drive the upper bound and median estimate. Key uncertainties include revenue run rate, customer concentration, and competitive positioning within the crowded biotech software and diagnostics space—factors that explain the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($600M to $3.3B).
